events.action_type: formal activation of the national security system
first confirmed case and explicit shift in pandemic-phase posture
creation of the Ad Hoc Cabinet Committee on the Covid-19 Response
activation of the National Crisis Management Centre at the Ministry of Health
appointment of an All-of-Government Controller to connect the national security system and the operational response
14-day self-isolation for arrivals and strengthened border-health measures announced
replacement of the Ad Hoc Cabinet Committee by the Covid-19 Ministerial Group with urgent decision authority
closure of the border to most non-citizens and non-residents

events.action_type: announcement of the four-stage Alert Level framework
move to Alert Level 3 with transition to Level 4 in 48 hours announced

events.action_type: declaration of a state of national emergency and entry into Level 4 lockdown
events.public_information_marker: alert-level communication; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: Quin-centred all-of-government response structure visible in National Action Plan 2.0
all-of-government plan for managing stranded people formalized in Cabinet material
additional Alert Level 4 rules clarified through Police guidance
events.public_information_marker: official guidance update; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: welfare coordination for people in quarantine and managed isolation formalized
guidance on the use of CDEM Act key powers updated for the COVID-19 state of national emergency
review-preparation for New Zealand's Level 4 status formalized in Cabinet material
Aviation Security and Police continue joint reassurance patrols and quarantine/isolation compliance checks
Alert Level 3 restrictions announced
events.public_information_marker: alert-level communication; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: date for move to Alert Level 3 publicly announced
implementation and compliance framework for the move to Alert Level 3 detailed in Cabinet material
Police confirm visible compliance, cross-agency support, and education-first enforcement during extended Level 4
events.public_information_marker: official guidance update; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: local-authority and CDEM welfare-support criteria widened to meet immediate COVID-19 response needs
Police clarify position on community checkpoints and partnership conditions with local authorities, CDEM managers, iwi, and communities
powers and authorisations to give effect to Alert Level 3 are formalized in Cabinet material
move to Alert Level 3 and first easing from Level 4 begins
events.public_information_marker: alert-level communication; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: Police urge the public to keep following the rules at Alert Level 3 as the easing interval begins
events.public_information_marker: official guidance update; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: Police remind the public of Alert Level 3 restrictions and continued compliance expectations
events.public_information_marker: official guidance update; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: preparation to review New Zealand's Level 3 status is formalized in Cabinet material
formal preparation for Alert Level 2 sets guidance revisions, service phasing, and public messaging expectations ahead of the next review
Police remind the public New Zealand remains in Alert Level 3 ahead of the next transition decision
events.public_information_marker: official guidance update; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: review of Alert Level 3 is completed in Cabinet paper and minute ahead of the move to Level 2
public decision announcement for the move to Alert Level 2
national transition period planning formalized in a joint DPMC/NEMA briefing
legal framework for COVID-19 Alert Level 2 passes
events.public_information_marker: official guidance update; enforcement or compliance communication

events.action_type: end of the state of national emergency and beginning of the national transition period
move to Alert Level 2 and start of the lower-acuity comparator window
move to Alert Level 1 and end of the national transition period
activation of the CECC and cross-agency command-centre support
first imported case is identified through onboard quarantine and routed to a designated hospital with local-health-authority notification
Taoyuan Airport Response Center is formed to implement CECC border-quarantine measures at the main point of entry
entry home-quarantine requirements are tightened for Hong Kong and Macau transit and for travelers returning from specified epidemic areas
clarification of home-quarantine support, daily follow-up, and airport/port notification expectations
restriction of foreign entry and routing of travelers into home-quarantine procedure through local district offices
suspension of airline passenger transit through Taiwan
clarification of home-quarantine and home-isolation rules, local care/support routing, and sanction language
health agencies are notified during home quarantine, medical care is arranged, and household contacts are placed into home isolation in confirmed imported-case handling
people under home quarantine are prohibited from domestic flight or ferry travel to offshore islands and the passenger-transit ban is extended to April 30
cumulative penalties and group-quarantine escalation clarified for home quarantine and home isolation breaches
phased social-distancing measures are announced with voluntary guidance first and conditional mandatory rules if needed later
symptomatic inbound travelers at airport or port routed by epidemic-prevention taxis to designated testing and medical-evaluation locations starting April 3
inbound travelers from Europe and the Americas required to notify health officials before return and use quarantine hotels when home conditions are unsuitable
extension of direct-flight restrictions and passenger-transit ban
entry response measures convened for travelers from outbreak areas including expert meeting and differentiated entry procedures
Entry Quarantine System launched for passenger health declaration and home quarantine information system to expedite immigration clearance
outbreak response measures launched for 48th confirmed case including epidemiological investigation and contact tracing
travel notice raised to Level 3 for Schengen countries with mandatory 14-day home quarantine for arriving travelers
20 imported cases response with epidemiological investigation, contact tracing, and quarantine reinforcement for Navy cluster
AHPPC first COVID statement addressing false assumptions about Chinese appearance and community concern
AHPPC travel restrictions resolution advising against travel to China
AHPPC pandemic risk escalation — sustained community transmission assessment in multiple countries
National Cabinet cruise ship ban and 500-person non-essential gathering limits
AHPPC trajectory comparison showing Australia's position relative to other nations with growing overseas-acquired cases
National Cabinet second stage activation with further business closures and enhanced enforcement
National Cabinet tenancies and health supply arrangements for returned travellers and community outbreaks
National Cabinet childcare and commercial tenancies coordination
AHPPC asymptomatic testing guidance and policy refinement
AHPPC aged care recommendations for residential facilities outbreak preparedness
AHPPC advice to National Cabinet on state/territory measures recognizing local circumstances for additional controls
AHPPC statement on COVID-19 in children advising preemptive closures not proportionate while acknowledging vulnerability
National Cabinet churches and worship places coordination for Easter services live streaming
AHPPC ANZAC Day guidance for vulnerable population protection and ceremony modifications
AHPPC health risk management guidance as measures lift and transition planning begins
National Cabinet maritime crew exemption and border logistics coordination for essential shipping
National Cabinet May meeting establishing monitoring, testing, and tracing framework for reopening
National Cabinet press conference announcing reopening framework and Australia-New Zealand travel bubble exploration
